sanskrit: तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम्। पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम्॥
transliteration: tasmāt praṇamya praṇidhāya kāyaṃ prasādaye tvām aham īśam īḍyam / piteva putrasya sakheva sakhyuḥ priyaḥ priyāyārhasi deva soḍhum
literal_meaning: Therefore, having bowed down and prostrated my body, I seek grace from You, the adorable Lord. Like a father with a son, a friend with a friend, a lover with the beloved, O God, please bear with me.
contextual_meaning: After seeing the terrifying cosmic form, Arjuna’s ego-defenses collapse; he shifts from bargaining to surrender, requesting relational repair through familiar attachment patterns.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic humility: ego disintegration followed by re-parenting fantasy—seeking absolution within safe relational frames.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack or psychedelic trip that shatters one’s self-image, texting a mentor: ‘I don’t know what I said/did—please don’t leave me, I need you to still see me as your friend.’
anchor_text: Collapse your pride and surrender to the divine like a child seeking a parent's embrace.
